methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $37M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 5.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Zeta Surgical

data.narrative:
Nabla Bio is a Series A biotech company founded by Surge Biswas and Frances Anastassacos that has raised $37M in equity plus $1.5B in partnership commitments. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 11 private and public peers in Biotech & Health, including 6 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies (Eikon Therapeutics, Neko Health, New Limit, Asimov, Profluent) and 3 public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression anchors on the $37M raised as a proxy for post-money valuation, applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $74M. Peer-median valuation across the 6 same-sector private comparables at identical or adjacent stages produces $805M, with a range spanning $100M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales is not applicable due to absence of target revenue data, a constraint typical of private biotech companies. 

The triangulated valuation range is $63M (low) to $926M (high), with a median of $440M across the two applicable methods. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around stage-appropriate valuation anchors and peer heterogeneity in this nascent sector. Stage-progression yields the most conservative estimate; peer-median heavily weights larger Series B rounds. Key sensitivities include Nabla's partnership terms, IP portfolio composition, and clinical-stage progress, none of which are quantified in available data.
